prompt: Set up a ChargeBay Operations Console that enables EV charging site teams to run reservations, bay occupancy validation, and session-to-order billing entirely offline in a responsive single-page web app. The React (TypeScript) interface uses React Router for kiosk-mode and admin-mode navigation, with a Table-driven "Sessions & Orders" workspace, a Reservation Drawer for quick check-in actions, and Modal flows for exceptions and compensation approvals. Users include System Administrator (global configuration and security), Site Manager (daily operations and pricing), Attendant (check-in/occupancy handling), and Auditor (read-only access to immutable logs and reports). For each reservation, attendants can confirm arrival via a manual confirmation step plus an optional QR scan from a locally generated code; if arrival is not confirmed within 10 minutes after the scheduled start time, the system auto-cancels as no-show and frees the bay. Temporary leave rules are configurable per site (default: 1 temporary leave up to 15 minutes); exceeding limits triggers an occupancy anomaly flagged as "Unverified Occupancy" after 30 minutes without an active session heartbeat update. An in-app Notification Center provides template-based messages for holds available, due reminders (15 minutes before), overdue alerts (at start+5 minutes), and approval outcomes, with per-role preference settings, read/archive status, and retry of failed local delivery attempts (e.g., rendering/queue failures) up to 3 times. All data is processed and persisted locally with IndexedDB as the primary "database" and LocalStorage for small settings (theme, last site, notification preferences); no network connectivity, third-party services, email, SMS, maps, or online payments are used. A frontend service layer validates and normalizes data, supporting Excel/CSV bulk import using downloadable templates, field mapping, pre-import validation (required fields, type checks, date format MM/DD/YYYY, pricing bounds), and full rollback if any row fails; deduplication rules default to "Station ID + Connector ID + Start Time" and "Order Number unique within site." Data quality governance runs on a local schedule (default weekly) to produce completeness and duplication reports viewable in-app and exportable as CSV via browser Blob/FileSaver downloads. Storage is optimized with client-side indexing (by site, bay, time range, status) and "hot/cold" tiering: the most recent 90 days remain in an active IndexedDB store while older records are moved to an archived store and can be exported as signed JSON packages for offline transfer between devices. Writes are idempotent using client-generated UUIDs, and records are versioned as raw/cleaned/aggregated to keep traceability from imported rows to reconciled billing results. Role-based access control is enforced at route/menu/button/field and row scope (site-based data scope), with every permission change and sensitive action (price edits, compensation approvals, reconciliation status transitions) appended to a tamper-evident local audit log; credentials are verified locally with salted password hashing (minimum 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes) and sensitive fields (customer name, plate, invoice notes) encrypted at rest using WebCrypto. Although the data model and API shapes are designed to be compatible with a NestJS + PostgreSQL deployment, this deliverable runs fully standalone in the browser and implements "integrations" only through locally signed import/export packages with signature verification and basic rate limiting on high-volume actions (e.g., bulk status updates capped at 200 rows per minute per user).
